name: SOC 2 Type II
description: Service Organization Control 2 (SOC 2) is a framework for managing customer data based on five Trust Service Criteria: security, availability, processing integrity, confidentiality, and privacy.

categories.name: Common Criteria (Security)
categories.description: Common criteria related to security, the foundation of all Trust Service Criteria.

controls.name: COSO Principle 1
controls.description: The entity demonstrates a commitment to integrity and ethical values.

requirements.description: Sets the tone at the top - The board of directors and management, at all levels, demonstrate through their directives, actions, and behavior the importance of integrity and ethical values.
requirements.guidance: Review code of conduct, board meeting minutes, and management communications for evidence of ethical tone.

requirements.description: Establishes standards of conduct - The expectations of the board of directors and senior management concerning integrity and ethical values are defined in the entity's standards of conduct.
requirements.guidance: Examine documented standards of conduct and employee acknowledgment records.

controls.name: COSO Principle 2
controls.description: The board of directors demonstrates independence from management and exercises oversight of the development and performance of internal control.

requirements.description: Establishes oversight responsibilities - The board of directors identifies and accepts its oversight responsibilities.
requirements.guidance: Review board charter and documented oversight responsibilities.

requirements.description: Applies relevant expertise - The board of directors defines, maintains, and periodically evaluates the skills and expertise needed among its members.
requirements.guidance: Examine board member qualifications and expertise assessments.

controls.name: COSO Principle 13
controls.description: The entity obtains or generates and uses relevant, quality information to support the functioning of internal control.

requirements.description: Identifies information requirements - A process is in place to identify the information required to support the functioning of internal control.
requirements.guidance: Review information requirements documentation and data flow diagrams.

requirements.description: Captures internal and external sources of data - Information systems capture internal and external sources of data.
requirements.guidance: Examine data capture mechanisms and source documentation.

controls.name: COSO Principle 6
controls.description: The entity specifies objectives with sufficient clarity to enable the identification and assessment of risks relating to objectives.

requirements.description: Operations objectives - Reflects management's choices and considers tolerance for risk.
requirements.guidance: Review documented operational objectives and risk tolerance statements.

requirements.description: External financial reporting objectives - Complies with applicable accounting standards.
requirements.guidance: Examine financial reporting objectives and compliance documentation.

controls.name: COSO Principle 16
controls.description: The entity selects, develops, and performs ongoing and/or separate evaluations to ascertain whether components of internal control are present and functioning.

requirements.description: Considers a mix of ongoing and separate evaluations - Management includes a balance of ongoing and separate evaluations.
requirements.guidance: Review monitoring activities schedule and evaluation reports.

requirements.description: Considers rate of change - Management considers the rate of change in business and processes when selecting evaluations.
requirements.guidance: Examine change management documentation and evaluation frequency assessments.

controls.name: COSO Principle 10
controls.description: The entity selects and develops control activities that contribute to the mitigation of risks to the achievement of objectives to acceptable levels.

requirements.description: Integrates with risk assessment - Control activities help ensure that risk responses are effectively carried out.
requirements.guidance: Review risk assessment documentation and control activity mapping.

requirements.description: Considers entity-specific factors - Management considers entity-specific factors when selecting control activities.
requirements.guidance: Examine control selection rationale and entity-specific considerations.

controls.name: Logical and Physical Access Controls
controls.description: The entity implements logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures over protected information assets.

requirements.description: Identifies and manages the inventory of information assets - The entity identifies, inventories, and manages information assets.
requirements.guidance: Review asset inventory and classification procedures.

requirements.description: Restricts logical access - Logical access to information assets is restricted through the use of access control software and rule sets.
requirements.guidance: Examine access control configurations and user provisioning processes.

requirements.description: Identifies and authenticates users - Users are identified and authenticated when accessing protected information assets.
requirements.guidance: Review authentication mechanisms and identity verification procedures.

controls.name: User Registration and Authorization
controls.description: Prior to issuing system credentials, the entity registers and authorizes new internal and external users.

requirements.description: Controls access credentials - Procedures are in place to control access credentials.
requirements.guidance: Review credential management policies and provisioning workflows.

requirements.description: Removes access when no longer required - Access to information assets is removed when no longer required.
requirements.guidance: Examine termination procedures and access removal logs.

controls.name: Role-Based Access
controls.description: The entity authorizes, modifies, or removes access based on roles, responsibilities, or the system design and changes.

requirements.description: Creates or modifies access based on authorization - Access is created or modified based on valid access authorization.
requirements.guidance: Review access request and approval workflows.

requirements.description: Reviews access periodically - Access is reviewed periodically and modified as needed.
requirements.guidance: Examine access review schedules and remediation actions.

controls.name: System Operations Monitoring
controls.description: To meet its objectives, the entity uses detection and monitoring procedures to identify changes to configurations and anomalies.

requirements.description: Uses defined configuration standards - Management has defined configuration standards.
requirements.guidance: Review configuration baseline documentation and hardening standards.

requirements.description: Monitors infrastructure components - Infrastructure components are monitored for deviations from standards.
requirements.guidance: Examine monitoring tools and alerting configurations.

controls.name: Security Event Monitoring
controls.description: The entity monitors system components and the operation of those components for anomalies indicative of malicious acts and errors.

requirements.description: Implements detection policies - Management implements detection policies and procedures.
requirements.guidance: Review security monitoring policies and SIEM configurations.

requirements.description: Monitors detection systems - Security detection systems are monitored for anomalies.
requirements.guidance: Examine security event logs and incident detection procedures.

controls.name: Change Management
controls.description: The entity authorizes, designs, develops, configures, documents, tests, approves, and implements changes to meet its objectives.

requirements.description: Manages changes throughout the system lifecycle - Changes are managed throughout the system lifecycle.
requirements.guidance: Review change management procedures and change request documentation.

requirements.description: Authorizes changes - Changes to the system are authorized before implementation.
requirements.guidance: Examine change approval workflows and authorization records.

requirements.description: Tests changes - Changes are tested before implementation.
requirements.guidance: Review test plans and test results documentation.

controls.name: Risk Mitigation
controls.description: The entity identifies, selects, and develops risk mitigation activities for risks arising from potential business disruptions.

requirements.description: Identifies risks that could impair objectives - Management identifies risks that could impair system objectives.
requirements.guidance: Review risk identification procedures and risk register.

requirements.description: Assesses risks to objectives - Identified risks are assessed to determine impact and likelihood.
requirements.guidance: Examine risk assessment methodology and results.

categories.name: Availability
categories.description: The system is available for operation and use as committed or agreed.

controls.name: Availability Commitments
controls.description: The entity maintains, monitors, and evaluates current processing capacity and use of system components to manage capacity demand.

requirements.description: Measures current usage - Current usage is measured to establish a baseline.
requirements.guidance: Review capacity monitoring tools and usage reports.

requirements.description: Forecasts capacity - Capacity forecasts are made based on historical trends and future requirements.
requirements.guidance: Examine capacity planning documentation and forecasting methods.

controls.name: Recovery Planning
controls.description: The entity authorizes, designs, develops, implements, operates, approves, maintains, and monitors environmental protections and recovery infrastructure.

requirements.description: Identifies environmental threats - Environmental threats are identified and addressed.
requirements.guidance: Review environmental risk assessments and mitigation controls.

requirements.description: Implements recovery infrastructure - Recovery infrastructure is in place to support system availability.
requirements.guidance: Examine backup and recovery infrastructure documentation.

controls.name: Recovery Testing
controls.description: The entity tests recovery plan procedures supporting system recovery to meet its objectives.

requirements.description: Tests recovery procedures - Recovery procedures are tested periodically.
requirements.guidance: Review recovery test plans and test results.

requirements.description: Documents test results - Recovery test results are documented and gaps addressed.
requirements.guidance: Examine recovery test reports and remediation actions.

categories.name: Processing Integrity
categories.description: System processing is complete, valid, accurate, timely, and authorized to meet the entity's objectives.

controls.name: Processing Integrity Policies
controls.description: The entity obtains or generates, uses, and communicates relevant, quality information regarding the objectives related to processing.

requirements.description: Defines processing specifications - Processing specifications are defined and documented.
requirements.guidance: Review processing specifications and data flow documentation.

requirements.description: Implements input controls - Input controls ensure data completeness and accuracy.
requirements.guidance: Examine input validation controls and error handling procedures.

controls.name: System Inputs
controls.description: The entity implements policies and procedures over system inputs that result in products, services, and reporting to meet the entity's objectives.

requirements.description: Validates system inputs - System inputs are validated for completeness and accuracy.
requirements.guidance: Review input validation rules and rejection handling.

controls.name: Processing Activities
controls.description: The entity implements policies and procedures over processing activities to result in products, services, and reporting to meet the entity's objectives.

requirements.description: Implements processing controls - Processing controls ensure transactions are processed correctly.
requirements.guidance: Examine processing logic and control procedures.

categories.name: Confidentiality
categories.description: Information designated as confidential is protected as committed or agreed.

controls.name: Confidentiality Policies
controls.description: The entity identifies and maintains confidential information to meet the entity's objectives related to confidentiality.

requirements.description: Identifies confidential information - Procedures exist to identify confidential information.
requirements.guidance: Review data classification policies and confidential data inventories.

requirements.description: Protects confidential information - Confidential information is protected from unauthorized access.
requirements.guidance: Examine access controls and encryption mechanisms for confidential data.

controls.name: Confidential Information Disposal
controls.description: The entity disposes of confidential information to meet the entity's objectives related to confidentiality.

requirements.description: Identifies data for disposal - Data requiring disposal is identified based on retention policies.
requirements.guidance: Review data retention policies and disposal schedules.

requirements.description: Disposes of data securely - Confidential data is disposed of using secure methods.
requirements.guidance: Examine secure disposal procedures and destruction certificates.

categories.name: Privacy
categories.description: Personal information is collected, used, retained, disclosed, and disposed to meet the entity's objectives.

controls.name: Privacy Notice
controls.description: The entity provides notice to data subjects about its privacy practices.

requirements.description: Provides notice at collection - Notice is provided at or before the time of collection.
requirements.guidance: Review privacy notices and collection point implementations.

controls.name: Consent
controls.description: The entity communicates choices available to data subjects and obtains consent.

requirements.description: Communicates choices - Choices regarding data use are communicated to data subjects.
requirements.guidance: Examine consent mechanisms and preference management.

requirements.description: Obtains consent - Consent is obtained before collecting personal information.
requirements.guidance: Review consent collection processes and documentation.

controls.name: Collection
controls.description: Personal information is collected consistent with the entity's objectives related to privacy.

requirements.description: Limits collection to specified purposes - Collection is limited to purposes identified in the privacy notice.
requirements.guidance: Review data collection practices against stated purposes.

controls.name: Use and Retention
controls.description: The entity limits the use and retention of personal information to meet the entity's objectives.

requirements.description: Limits use to purposes - Use is limited to purposes identified in the privacy notice.
requirements.guidance: Examine data use practices and purpose limitation controls.

requirements.description: Retains for required period - Personal information is retained only for the required period.
requirements.guidance: Review retention schedules and data lifecycle management.

controls.name: Access
controls.description: The entity grants identified and authenticated data subjects the ability to access their stored personal information.

requirements.description: Authenticates data subject - Data subject identity is authenticated before providing access.
requirements.guidance: Review identity verification procedures for data subject requests.

requirements.description: Provides access within reasonable time - Access is provided within a reasonable time frame.
requirements.guidance: Examine data subject request handling procedures and timelines.
